page.title: Medicare Savings Programs in Utah {year} — QMB, SLMB, QI | Vernal Medicare
page.heading: Medicare Savings Programs (QMB, SLMB, QI)
page.description: Medicare Savings Programs can pay your Part B premium — and QMB pays much more. See {year} Utah income and resource limits for QMB, SLMB, and QI, and how to apply.
page.summary: Medicare Savings Programs (MSPs) are state-run programs that help pay your Medicare costs. QMB pays your Part A & B premiums plus deductibles and coinsurance; SLMB and QI pay your Part B premium ({partBPremium}/mo in {year}). Qualifying also unlocks Extra Help for drugs automatically.

faqs.question: What is the difference between QMB, SLMB, and QI?
faqs.answer: All three help pay Medicare costs, with rising income limits. QMB pays the most — your Part A and Part B premiums plus deductibles, coinsurance, and copays. SLMB and QI pay your Part B premium ({partBPremium}/month in {year}). QI has limited funding, so apply early in the year.

faqs.question: What are the {year} income limits in Utah?
faqs.answer: Monthly income limits for {year} are roughly {qmbIndividual} (QMB), {slmbIndividual} (SLMB), and {qiIndividual} (QI) for a single person, and higher for couples. Countable resources must be under about {resIndividual} (single) or {resCouple} (married). Limits are higher than many expect.

faqs.question: Does an MSP also help with drug costs?
faqs.answer: Yes. If you qualify for any Medicare Savings Program, you automatically qualify for Extra Help (the Part D Low-Income Subsidy), which lowers or eliminates your prescription drug premium, deductible, and copays.

sources.title: Utah Medicaid / Department of Workforce Services

nextSteps.when: If your monthly income is near these limits,
nextSteps.action: apply anyway — the limits are higher than most people expect, and qualifying also unlocks Extra Help. We can check all of it for free.

meta.ogTitle: Medicare Savings Programs (QMB, SLMB, QI) — Utah
meta.ogDescription: {year} Utah income & resource limits for QMB, SLMB, and QI, and how they help pay your Medicare costs.

body.introHtml: If Medicare's monthly premiums and out-of-pocket costs strain your budget, a Medicare Savings Program (MSP) may cover them for you. These are state-run programs — in Utah, through Medicaid / the Department of Workforce Services — and qualifying also unlocks Extra Help for prescriptions automatically.

programs.name: Qualified Medicare Beneficiary
programs.pays: Part A & B premiums, plus deductibles, coinsurance, and copays

programs.name: Qualifying Individual
programs.pays: Part B premium (limited funds — apply early each year)

body.resourceNoteHtml: Resource (asset) limits for {year}: about {resIndividual} for a single person and {resCouple} for a married couple. Your home and one car generally don't count.
body.worthHtml: QMB is especially valuable — it can eliminate your Part B premium ({partBPremium}/month in {year}) and cover deductibles and coinsurance, effectively acting like a supplement at no cost to you. Even SLMB or QI, which "only" pay the Part B premium, put real money back in your pocket every month.
body.outroHtml: Because income limits are higher than most people assume — and because qualifying automatically triggers Extra Help for drugs — it's almost always worth checking. Start with the dual-eligible overview, or call us and we'll check every program you might qualify for at once.
